title: potentilla hispanica subsp. ibrahimiana
intro: Potentilla hispanica subsp. ibrahimiana, also known as the Turkish potentilla, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Potentilla hispanica subsp. ibrahimiana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea, or the leaves and flowers are used in infusions, tinctures, and salves, highlighting its versatility in traditional medicine.
